events.title: Beef Stir-Fry with Vegetables
events.description:
🍽️  Beef Stir-Fry with Vegetables

🌍 Cuisine: Chinese / A
 sian Fusion
📍 Origin: Guangdong Province, Southern China

📖 The St
 ory:
Stir-frying was developed in China as a way to cook small amounts 
 of food quickly over high heat with minimal fuel. This technique became 
 essential in Southern China where fuel was scarce. The 'wok hei' or 'bre
 ath of the wok' is that smoky char flavor achieved only at high temperat
 ures.

⏱️  Timing:
   Prep: 15 min
   Cook: 10 min
   Total: 25 min
 
   Difficulty: Easy
   Serves: 4

🛒 Ingredients:
   • 1.5 lbs rib
 eye steak, thinly sliced against the grain
   • 3 cups mixed vegetable
 s (broccoli, bell peppers, snap peas)
   • 3 tbsp vegetable oil
   •
  3 cloves garlic, minced
   • 1 tbsp fresh ginger, grated
   • 1/4 c
 up soy sauce
   • 2 tbsp oyster sauce
   • 1 tbsp sesame oil
   • 1 t
 sp cornstarch
   • 1/4 cup beef broth
   • 2 green onions, sliced
  
  • 1 tbsp sesame seeds
   • Cooked rice for serving

👨‍🍳 Instructio
 ns:
   1. Slice beef thinly against the grain. Toss with 1 tbsp soy sau
 ce and set aside.
   2. Mix remaining soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame
  oil, cornstarch, and beef broth in a bowl.
   3. Heat wok or large s
 killet over high heat until smoking.
   4. Add 2 tbsp oil and swirl to 
 coat. Add beef in a single layer and sear for 1-2 minutes.
   5. Remove
  beef from wok and set aside.
   6. Add remaining oil, garlic, and gi
 nger. Stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
   7. Add vegetables and 
 stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until crisp-tender.
   8. Return beef to wok. 
 Pour sauce over and toss constantly for 1-2 minutes until thickened.
  
  9. Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds. Serve over rice.

🎥 H
 ow to Cook This Recipe:
   Watch on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/wa
 tch?v=IYO7CLvh23U

💡 Chef's Tip:
   High heat is key! Have all ingre
 dients prepped before starting. The wok should be smoking hot.

🍷 Win
 e Pairing: Cabernet Sauvignon or Malbec

💰 Estimated Cost: $24-28

events.title: Mediterranean Chicken Bowl
events.description:
🍽️  Mediterranean Chicken Bowl

🌍 Cuisine: Mediterranean 
 / Greek
📍 Origin: Greece and the Levant

📖 The Story:
The Mediterr
 anean diet has been celebrated for centuries as one of the healthiest in
  the world. This bowl brings together the bright flavors of the region -
  lemon, olive oil, fresh herbs, and tangy feta. Greek islanders have 
 been eating variations of this for millennia, using whatever was fresh 
 from the land and sea.

⏱️  Timing:
   Prep: 15 min
   Cook: 20 min\
 n   Total: 35 min
   Difficulty: Easy
   Serves: 4

🛒 Ingredients:\
 n   • 2 lbs chicken breast, cubed
   • 1 tsp dried oregano
   • 1 tsp
  garlic powder
   • 2 tbsp olive oil
   • 2 cups quinoa
   • 4 cups c
 hicken broth
   • 2 cucumbers, diced
   • 1 pint cherry tomatoes, ha
 lved
   • 1/2 red onion, diced
   • 8 oz feta cheese, crumbled
   •
  1/2 cup hummus
   • 1/4 cup kalamata olives
   • 2 tbsp fresh lemon j
 uice
   • Fresh parsley for garnish
   • Tzatziki for serving

👨‍🍳
  Instructions:
   1. Season chicken cubes with oregano, garlic powder\
 , salt, and pepper.
   2. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over mediu
 m-high heat.
   3. Cook chicken for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionall
 y, until golden and cooked through.
   4. Meanwhile, rinse quinoa and
  cook in chicken broth according to package directions.
   5. Make cucu
 mber-tomato salad: Combine cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, and lemon j
 uice.
   6. Fluff cooked quinoa with a fork.
   7. Assemble bowls: Qui
 noa base, chicken, cucumber salad, hummus dollop.
   8. Top with fet
 a, olives, and fresh parsley.
   9. Serve with tzatziki on the side.\
 n
🎥 How to Cook This Recipe:
   Watch on YouTube: https://www.youtube
 .com/watch?v=5h9q1FiD5s0

💡 Chef's Tip:
   Toast the quinoa in a dry
  pan before cooking for extra nutty flavor.

🍷 Wine Pairing: Assyrtik
 o or Pinot Grigio

💰 Estimated Cost: $26-30

events.title: Korean Beef Bulgogi Bowl
events.description:
🍽️  Korean Beef Bulgogi Bowl

🌍 Cuisine: Korean
📍 Origi
 n: Seoul, South Korea (Ancient origins, modern form 1940s)

📖 The S
 tory:
Bulgogi literally means 'fire meat' in Korean. Originally a dish 
 of the Korean royal court, it was traditionally prepared for the wealth
 y and nobility. The marinade of soy, pear, and sesame was developed to
  tenderize the meat in a time before refrigeration. Today it's Korea's m
 ost beloved dish, enjoyed at BBQ restaurants worldwide.

⏱️  Timing:\
 n   Prep: 20 min (+2 hrs marinade)
   Cook: 15 min
   Total: 2 hrs 35 
 min
   Difficulty: Medium
   Serves: 4

🛒 Ingredients:
   • 1.5 lb
 s ribeye steak, thinly sliced
   • 1 Asian pear, grated (or 1/4 cup a
 pple juice)
   • 1/4 cup soy sauce
   • 2 tbsp brown sugar
   • 2 tbs
 p sesame oil
   • 3 cloves garlic, minced
   • 1 tbsp fresh ginger, 
 grated
   • 2 green onions, sliced
   • 1 tbsp sesame seeds
   • 1/2
  tsp black pepper
   • 2 cups jasmine rice
   • 16 oz kimchi
   • 1 c
 ucumber, sliced
   • 2 fried eggs for topping (optional)

👨‍🍳 Inst
 ructions:
   1. Make marinade: Mix grated pear, soy sauce, brown suga
 r, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, and pepper.
   2. Add sliced beef to
  marinade, toss to coat. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours, preferably 
 overnight.
   3. Cook jasmine rice according to package directions.
  
  4. Heat a large skillet or grill pan over high heat.
   5. Cook beef i
 n batches for 2-3 minutes per side until caramelized and cooked through.
 
   6. Sprinkle with green onions and sesame seeds.
   7. Assemble bow
 ls: Rice base, bulgogi, kimchi, cucumber slices.
   8. Top with a fr
 ied egg if desired. Serve with extra gochujang on the side.

🎥 How to
  Cook This Recipe:
   Watch on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v
 =Xnq0uG4bLdE

💡 Chef's Tip:
   The pear in the marinade is tradition
 al and helps tenderize the meat. Don't skip the marinade time!

🍷 Win
 e Pairing: Soju or light Pinot Noir

💰 Estimated Cost: $27-32

events.title: Grilled Salmon with Asparagus
events.description:
🍽️  Grilled Salmon with Asparagus

🌍 Cuisine: Pacific Nor
 thwest American
📍 Origin: Pacific Northwest, USA / Coastal Alaska


 📖 The Story:
Salmon has been the lifeblood of Pacific Northwest indige
 nous peoples for thousands of years. Modern Pacific Northwest cuisine ce
 lebrates this heritage with simple preparations that honor the fish. Gri
 lling over cedar planks (a native tradition) or directly on the grill wi
 th minimal seasoning lets the wild salmon's rich, fatty flavor take cen
 ter stage.

⏱️  Timing:
   Prep: 10 min
   Cook: 15 min
   Total: 2
 5 min
   Difficulty: Medium
   Serves: 4

🛒 Ingredients:
   • 4 sa
 lmon fillets (6 oz each), skin on
   • 1 lb asparagus, trimmed
   • 
 3 tbsp olive oil
   • 2 tbsp fresh lemon juice
   • 2 cloves garlic, 
 minced
   • 1 tsp fresh dill, chopped
   • 1 tsp fresh thyme leaves

    • 2 tbsp butter, melted
   • 1 lemon, sliced
   • Salt and pepper
  to taste

👨‍🍳 Instructions:
   1. Pat salmon dry and bring to room
  temperature for 15 minutes.
   2. Preheat grill to medium-high heat (4
 00°F).
   3. Make marinade: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, di
 ll, thyme, salt, and pepper.
   4. Brush salmon and asparagus with m
 arinade.
   5. Place salmon skin-side down on the grill. Cook for 5-6 m
 inutes.
   6. Flip salmon and add asparagus to grill. Cook for 3-4 more
  minutes.
   7. Salmon is done when it flakes easily with a fork (inter
 nal temp 145°F).
   8. Remove from grill and brush with melted butter.\
 n   9. Serve with lemon slices and extra fresh herbs.

🎥 How to Cook 
 This Recipe:
   Watch on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=J6xW4
 f_q34g

💡 Chef's Tip:
   Don't flip the salmon too early - it will r
 elease from the grill when ready. Skin helps protect the fish.

🍷 Win
 e Pairing: Pinot Noir or Chardonnay

💰 Estimated Cost: $28-32

events.title: Pan-Seared Cod with Lemon Butter
events.description:
🍽️  Pan-Seared Cod with Lemon Butter

🌍 Cuisine: Mediterr
 anean / Italian
📍 Origin: Coastal Italy and Greece

📖 The Story:
T
 his classic preparation has been enjoyed along the Mediterranean coast f
 or centuries. The simplicity of fresh fish with lemon and butter allows 
 the delicate flavor of the cod to shine through. Italian fishermen would
  cook their morning catch this way, using whatever herbs were growing i
 n their garden.

⏱️  Timing:
   Prep: 10 min
   Cook: 10 min
   Tot
 al: 20 min
   Difficulty: Easy
   Serves: 4

🛒 Ingredients:
   • 1
 .5 lbs cod fillets
   • 4 tbsp unsalted butter
   • 2 tbsp olive oil

    • 3 tbsp fresh lemon juice
   • 2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped
   •
  2 tbsp capers, drained
   • 1/4 cup white wine (optional)
   • 2 clo
 ves garlic, minced
   • Salt and pepper to taste
   • Lemon wedges fo
 r serving

👨‍🍳 Instructions:
   1. Pat cod fillets dry with paper t
 owels. Season both sides with salt and pepper.
   2. Heat olive oil in 
 a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
   3. Place cod
  in the skillet, presentation side down. Cook for 3-4 minutes until gol
 den brown.
   4. Flip the fillets and cook for another 2-3 minutes unti
 l fish flakes easily.
   5. Remove fish to a warm plate and tent with f
 oil.
   6. In the same pan, add butter and let it melt and foam. Add g
 arlic and sauté for 30 seconds.
   7. Add lemon juice, white wine (if 
 using), and capers. Simmer for 1-2 minutes.
   8. Stir in fresh parsle
 y and pour sauce over the fish.
   9. Serve immediately with lemon wedg
 es and your favorite sides.

🎥 How to Cook This Recipe:
   Watch on 
 YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tlr2X09QKeo

💡 Chef's Tip:

    Don't overcook the cod - it should flake easily but still be moist. T
 he sauce should be bright and tangy.

🍷 Wine Pairing: Sauvignon Blanc
  or Pinot Grigio

💰 Estimated Cost: $28-32

events.title: Fish Tacos with Mango Salsa
events.description:
🍽️  Fish Tacos with Mango Salsa

🌍 Cuisine: Mexican / Baj
 a California
📍 Origin: Baja California, Mexico (1980s)

📖 The Stor
 y:
Fish tacos originated in the coastal towns of Baja California, wher
 e local fishermen would cook their catch in simple batter and serve it i
 n tortillas with fresh salsa. The dish exploded in popularity in San Die
 go in the 1980s and has since become a California staple. The contrast o
 f crispy fish with sweet mango is pure coastal magic.

⏱️  Timing:
  
  Prep: 20 min
   Cook: 10 min
   Total: 30 min
   Difficulty: Easy
 
   Serves: 4

🛒 Ingredients:
   • 1.5 lbs white fish (tilapia or cod)
 , cut into strips
   • 8-10 corn tortillas
   • 2 ripe mangos, diced
 
   • 1/2 red onion, finely diced
   • 1 jalapeño, seeded and minced
 
   • 1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped
   • 2 limes, juiced
   • 1/2
  tsp salt
   • 8 oz cabbage slaw mix
   • 1/2 cup chipotle mayo
   • 
 1 cup all-purpose flour
   • 1 tsp chili powder
   • 1/2 tsp cumin
  
  • Vegetable oil for frying
   • Lime wedges for serving

👨‍🍳 Instr
 uctions:
   1. Make mango salsa: Combine mango, red onion, jalapeño,
  cilantro, lime juice, and salt. Set aside to meld.
   2. Mix flour w
 ith chili powder, cumin, and a pinch of salt in a shallow dish.
   3.
  Heat 1/2 inch of oil in a skillet to 350°F (175°C).
   4. Dredge fish 
 in seasoned flour, shake off excess.
   5. Fry fish in batches for 3-4
  minutes until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels.
   6. Warm tor
 tillas on a dry skillet or directly over a gas flame.
   7. Assemble ta
 cos: Spread chipotle mayo on tortilla, add slaw, crispy fish, and man
 go salsa.
   8. Squeeze fresh lime over each taco before serving.

🎥
  How to Cook This Recipe:
   Watch on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/
 watch?v=JkDXvK1_caI

💡 Chef's Tip:
   Don't overcrowd the pan when f
 rying - this lowers the oil temperature and makes fish soggy.

🍷 Wine
  Pairing: Mexican Lager or Sauvignon Blanc

💰 Estimated Cost: $26-30

events.title: Chicken Tikka Masala
events.description:
🍽️  Chicken Tikka Masala

🌍 Cuisine: Indian / British-Ind
 ian
📍 Origin: Glasgow, Scotland (1970s) - disputed

📖 The Story:

 The origin of Chicken Tikka Masala is hotly debated! Many claim it was i
 nvented in Glasgow, Scotland in the 1970s when a customer complained hi
 s chicken tikka was too dry. The chef added a can of tomato soup and cre
 am, creating the creamy orange sauce we know today. Others claim it has
  roots in Punjab. Either way, it's now Britain's national dish and belo
 ved worldwide.

⏱️  Timing:
   Prep: 15 min (+1 hr marinade)
   Cook
 : 35 min
   Total: 1 hr 50 min
   Difficulty: Medium
   Serves: 4


 🛒 Ingredients:
   • 2 lbs chicken breast, cut into 1-inch cubes
   •
  1 cup plain yogurt
   • 2 tbsp lemon juice
   • 2 tsp turmeric
   • 
 2 tsp garam masala
   • 2 tsp cumin
   • 1 tsp paprika
   • 1 tsp cay
 enne pepper
   • 2 tbsp vegetable oil
   • 1 large onion, diced
   •
  4 cloves garlic, minced
   • 1 tbsp fresh ginger, grated
   • 1 can
  (14 oz) tomato puree
   • 1 cup heavy cream
   • 1/2 cup chicken brot
 h
   • 2 tbsp butter
   • Fresh cilantro for garnish
   • Basmati ric
 e and naan for serving

👨‍🍳 Instructions:
   1. Marinate chicken: M
 ix yogurt, lemon juice, half the spices, and salt. Add chicken, coat
  well. Refrigerate 1+ hours.
   2. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large skillet. 
 Cook chicken in batches until browned. Set aside.
   3. In the same pan
 , add remaining oil and butter. Sauté onion until soft (5 min).
   4. 
 Add garlic, ginger, and remaining spices. Cook for 1 minute until frag
 rant.
   5. Add tomato puree and chicken broth. Simmer for 10 minutes.\
 n   6. Stir in heavy cream and return chicken to the pan.
   7. Simmer 
 gently for 15-20 minutes until chicken is cooked through and sauce thick
 ens.
   8. Taste and adjust seasoning. Garnish with fresh cilantro.
  
  9. Serve over basmati rice with warm naan.

🎥 How to Cook This Recip
 e:
   Watch on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ysUumkRFP8I


 💡 Chef's Tip:
   For extra authentic flavor, grill the marinated chic
 ken on skewers (tikka) before adding to the sauce.

🍷 Wine Pairing: R
 iesling or Gewürztraminer

💰 Estimated Cost: $22-26
